outcome_contract.why: Fresh deployments and CI builds fail silently (500 on every page) when the content generation step is skipped. The materializer warning also means even manual re-runs are unsafe without the trustStrip fix.
outcome_contract.intended_outcome_statement: The Caryina app builds and serves content on fresh checkout without a separate manual generation step; all pages fall back to hardcoded safe content rather than throwing a 500 when the file is absent or malformed.

engineering_coverage.applicable: Fallback returns defaults; shop renders with placeholder copy instead of 500
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Same fallback + prebuild keeps fresh content; fresh checkout works end-to-end
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Graceful degraded state on missing file; full state on normal build

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Same; also logs when materializer runs in prebuild
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Add `console.warn` in fallback path; CI logs capture prebuild run

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Same; also test that prebuild produces valid output
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Unit tests for `readPayload()` fallback; integration: prebuild + typecheck in CI

engineering_coverage.applicable: No change to materializer schema; trustStrip remains manual
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Materializer schema extended with `trustStrip`; app-local stale copy deleted; single canonical path
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Schema drift resolved; one authoritative copy

engineering_coverage.applicable: Single point of failure eliminated for missing-file case; malformed-file case also covered
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Same; additionally, fresh checkout produces correct content via prebuild
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Full resilience across missing + malformed + fresh checkout

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Materializer change is additive (new field added, not removed); committed file remains rollback baseline
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Rollback = revert the JSON commit; no migration needed

stage_payload.decision_summary: The Caryina shop throws 500 errors on every server-rendered page when `data/shops/caryina/site-content.generated.json` is absent. Generation is entirely manual, there is no prebuild hook, and the materializer currently cannot be run safely because `productPage.trustStrip` is not in its output schema (the field was added manually after generation). Two decisions need to be made: 1. **Resilience approach**: Should the app have a runtime fallback, a prebuild guard, or both? 2. **trustStrip gap**: Should we port `trustStrip` to the materializer (enabling automated prebuild) or keep it manual-only
goals: App never hard-crashes (500) when the content file is absent or malformed
No manual step required between fresh checkout and a successful build
Two-copy divergence resolved; single authoritative path
Unit tests cover the fallback paths
constraints: `trustStrip` in `productPage` must not be dropped; it is customer-visible content on every PDP
CI `caryina.yml` build command cannot be changed without a separate PR touching the workflow file
`prebuild` in `apps/caryina/package.json` runs with `cwd = apps/caryina/`; materializer needs `--repo-root ../..`
assumptions: The `## Reusable Trust Blocks` section in `HBAG/content-packet.md` is the correct source for trustStrip bullets — confirmed by reading the file (delivery, exchange, origin are all present)
The committed `data/shops/caryina/site-content.generated.json` (repo root) is the app's primary resolved path; the app-local copy at `apps/caryina/data/` is stale and safe to delete

evaluation_criteria.Why it matters: Core goal — entire shop is offline otherwise
Customer-visible on every PDP; must not be dropped by regeneration
Prevents regression to the same fragile state
Ensures fallback paths don't silently regress
Two-copy divergence is a maintenance hazard
Prebuild must not corrupt committed content

options_considered.Description: Add `SAFE_DEFAULTS` payload in `contentPacket.ts`; replace hard throws with `console.warn` + return defaults. No prebuild, no materializer change.
options_considered.Upside: Smallest change; ship fast; no risk of materializer overwriting content
options_considered.Downside: Does nothing to keep content fresh; fresh checkout still serves placeholder copy; trustStrip gap remains
options_considered.Key risks: Stale/missing content served silently in production after future deploys

options_considered.Description: Add `prebuild` script that runs materializer. Don't port trustStrip. Guard: skip regeneration if hash unchanged.
options_considered.Downside: Materializer would overwrite trustStrip on first changed-hash run; guard only delays problem
options_considered.Key risks: Loss of customer-visible PDP trust copy after any content-packet edit

options_considered.Description: (1) Port `trustStrip` to materializer `buildPayload()` from `## Reusable Trust Blocks`. (2) Add `prebuild` that runs materializer. (3) Add `SAFE_DEFAULTS` fallback in `readPayload()`. (4) Delete stale app-local copy. (5) Add unit tests.
options_considered.Upside: Solves all dimensions: no crash on fresh checkout; content stays fresh; no manual step; no content loss; tested
options_considered.Downside: More tasks than A alone; materializer change requires care to extract trust bullets correctly
options_considered.Key risks: If `## Reusable Trust Blocks` section is renamed or removed from content-packet, trustStrip would revert to defaults — acceptable since defaults are reasonable copy

options_considered.Description: Keep manual generation; add a proper Next.js error boundary that renders a useful "shop temporarily unavailable" page instead of a raw 500
options_considered.Upside: Honest failure mode; no content surprises
options_considered.Downside: Still requires manual step on fresh checkout; production can go offline silently; doesn't fix the root fragility
options_considered.Key risks: Operator must always remember to run generation before push/deploy

chosen_approach.Recommendation: Option C — Port trustStrip to materializer, add prebuild script, add runtime fallback, delete stale copy, add unit tests.
chosen_approach.Why this wins: Only approach that simultaneously eliminates the 500 crash (via fallback), eliminates the manual generation step (via prebuild), and avoids content loss (by porting trustStrip before the prebuild runs). Option A alone fixes the crash but leaves fresh checkout serving placeholder copy indefinitely. Option B is unsafe until trustStrip is ported. Option C is the minimal complete fix.
chosen_approach.`## Reusable Trust Blocks` section in `H: contains 3 bullet lines matching delivery/exchange/origin semantics already in `TRUST_STRIP_DEFAULTS`)
chosen_approach.**Option B (prebuild without trustStrip: any content-packet edit triggering a hash change would cause the materializer to regenerate and strip `productPage.trustStrip`, replacing customer-visible PDP trust copy with empty/placeholder content. Eliminated.

rejected_approaches: **Option A (fallback only)** — Fixes the 500 crash but fresh checkout still serves placeholder copy from `SAFE_DEFAULTS`. Does not fulfil the outcome contract ("builds and serves content on fresh checkout without a separate manual generation step"). Retained as a component of Option C.

**Option B (prebuild without trustStrip port)** — Unsafe: any content-packet edit triggering a hash change would cause the materializer to regenerate and strip `productPage.trustStrip`, replacing customer-visible PDP trust copy with empty/placeholder content. Eliminated.
**Option D (error boundary only)** — Fixes the presentation of failure but not the failure itself. Fresh checkout still requires a manual step. Does not fulfil the outcome contract.

sequencing_constraints: TASK-01 before TASK-03 (materializer must include trustStrip before prebuild run could overwrite the file)
TASK-02 before TASK-05 (fallback must exist before tests for it)
TASK-04 can be done any time (only removes a stale tracked file)

areas.label: Content generation
areas.current: Manual: operator runs materializer, commits result
areas.trigger: Developer runs `pnpm build` or pushes to CI
areas.future: `prebuild` in `apps/caryina/package.json` auto-runs materializer with `--repo-root ../..`; materializer reads `HBAG/content-packet.md`, writes `data/shops/caryina/site-content.generated.json`; `next build` then consumes the freshly-written file
areas.steady: Content-packet editing is still manual (out of scope); operator must still update `HBAG/content-packet.md` to change content
areas.seams: If content-packet is missing (corrupted checkout), materializer fails with `ok:false`; prebuild exits non-zero; build fails with a clear diagnostic (better than silent 500)

areas.label: Runtime resilience
areas.current: Hard throw + 500 on any missing/malformed file
areas.trigger: SSR page render when file absent
areas.future: `readPayload()` logs a warning and returns `SAFE_DEFAULTS`; all `get*Content()` functions return safe fallback copy; pages render with degraded but non-crashing content
areas.steady: File is still read on first call; cache logic unchanged
areas.seams: Fallback copy must be real brand copy, not generator placeholders, to avoid customer-facing quality issues

areas.label: Two-copy divergence
areas.future: `git rm apps/caryina/data/shops/caryina/site-content.generated.json`; only `data/shops/caryina/site-content.generated.json` remains; `GENERATED_PAYLOAD_CANDIDATES` still checks all three paths (no code change needed)
areas.seams: None; app-local copy was never preferred by the candidate list

areas.label: Materializer schema
areas.future: `buildPayload()` extracts trust bullets from `## Reusable Trust Blocks` in content-packet; maps to `{ delivery, exchange, origin }` matching `TRUST_STRIP_DEFAULTS` shape; `securePayment` falls back to default
areas.steady: `trustStrip` remains optional in consumer (`SiteContentPayload`); existing `mergeLocalizedSection` handling unchanged
areas.seams: `## Reusable Trust Blocks` section shape must remain stable in content-packet; if renamed, trustStrip reverts to defaults (acceptable)

areas.label: Test coverage
areas.current: Zero tests for missing/invalid file paths
areas.future: Unit tests in `contentPacket.test.ts` cover: (a) missing file → returns `SAFE_DEFAULTS`, (b) malformed JSON → returns `SAFE_DEFAULTS`
areas.seams: Tests run in CI only; must follow `fs.existsSync`/`fs.readFileSync` mock pattern
